structured_fields.name: diagnostic_criteria
structured_fields.description: Recognised diagnostic criteria — reference established classification systems where they exist (e.g. ACR, EULAR, DSM). Each criterion as a concise string.
example: ACR/EULAR 2019: score ≥10 with positive ANA as entry criterion
Malar rash or acute cutaneous lupus

structured_fields.name: laboratory_tests
structured_fields.description: Key laboratory tests used in diagnosis — each with a brief description of purpose

example.description: Primary screening test — positive in >95% of SLE patients
Highly specific for SLE — correlates with disease activity and nephritis

structured_fields.name: imaging_pathology
structured_fields.description: Imaging modalities and pathology investigations used in diagnosis or monitoring

freetext_fields.name: differential_diagnosis
freetext_fields.description: Narrative covering key conditions that must be ruled out during diagnosis — explain distinguishing features and how clinicians differentiate them from this disease

freetext_fields.name: screening_early_detection
freetext_fields.description: Narrative on current screening approaches, early detection strategies and any gaps in early diagnosis — particularly in high risk populations

prompt_hints.tone: precise and clinical — reference established guidelines and classification systems where possible
avoid: inventing diagnostic criteria — only reference established systems
listing tests without explaining their diagnostic relevance
vague differential diagnosis statements without distinguishing features
prefer: naming the specific classification system for diagnostic criteria
ordering laboratory tests from most to least diagnostically significant
differential diagnosis that reflects real clinical challenge not obvious exclusions
